raw_text:
```json
{
  "claims": [
    "SB 1427 creates a competitive grant program for counties to establish or expand 'homeless and mental health courts'.",
    "SB 1427 allows counties to institute re-entry services for jail inmates at risk of becoming homeless upon release.",
    "California counties have collaborative courts to improve outcomes for specialized populations of criminal offenders.",
    "Mental Health Courts operate in 44 counties and homeless courts in 13 counties in California.",
    "Collaborative courts provide individuals with mental illness and homeless individuals access to resources needed to rehabilitate their lives.",
    "Collaborative courts aim to reduce prison and jail crowding by promoting treatment over custody.",
    "Numerous reviews have found that Mental Health Courts reduce incarceration and re-offense rates.",
    "A 2017 study of the Sacramento County Mental Health Court found a 70 percent completion rate among participants.",
    "Mental Health Court participants had a lower rate of re-arrest compared to non-graduates.",
    "Mental Health Court participants had a 75 percent lower chance of being hospitalized.",
    "Mental Health Courts have shown a decrease in re-offense across various demographics."
  ]
}
```
